mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits lower discount rates to equities and supports risk appetite, so a downward month-over-month move in policy rates predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive more often than negative when FEDFUNDS is trending down, or if walk-forward Rank IC stays below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration forces tighter expected policy and higher real discount rates, which compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change rises above 3.0% and FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%
regime_hint: early inflation pickup with low policy rates
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances do not show negative 20-day SPX returns at least 55% of the time, or if the signal flips positive in repeated inflation-up episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income and demand, so a labor-market deterioration over recent months predicts lower S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not negative in most 0.3pp UNRATE rise episodes, or if the rolling 2-year hit rate falls below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: Improving retail sales growth in RSAFS reflects stronger current spending and revenue expectations, which supports equities and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS month-over-month growth does not align with positive 20-day SPX returns, or if Rank IC remains below 0.03 across any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO together with stable PAYEMS indicates broad production growth without labor stress, which supports earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if joint INDPRO and PAYEMS improvements fail to outperform either series alone, or if triggered positive-production episodes do not generate positive 20-day returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST signals housing demand weakness and a forthcoming slowdown in cyclical spending, so a sharp housing-starts decline predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 8% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day forward SPX returns are not negative after large HOUST drops, or if the signal shows no edge relative to unconditional returns over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth weakens while UNRATE rises, the labor market is deteriorating in a way that directly reduces earnings growth expectations and lowers S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth turns negative while UNRATE rises at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor deterioration does not outperform single-variable signals, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not negative in at least 60% of triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A negative GDPC1 print after prior growth indicates broad demand contraction that weakens corporate revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Reject if negative GDPC1 growth does not precede negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect disappears in subsequent quarterly releases after accounting for revision risk.
